tasks.title: Core Systems Architecture Spec

The two accepted proposals define what the game does — this spec defines how the systems that make it work are structured.

tasks.title: Purchase Feedback & Rate Visibility
tasks.overview:
Every button press in the game — buying generators, purchasing upgrades — feels imperceptible. The player can't tell if anything happened.

This has been verified by directly observing the running game at localhost:5173. The economy is working correctly — one selfies generator produces ~6.8 engagement/sec passively, which is meaningful. But the UI is completely silent: no rate indicator, no button feedback, no animation on numbers, no indication that a generator is running after purchase.

The root cause is a visibility problem: the player watches a raw total accumulate with no rate surfaced, so they can never perceive that their rate changed. The numbers are fine. This is a pure UX problem.

Specific gaps observed:
- No rate indicator (e.g. +6.8/sec) anywhere in the UI
- No visual feedback on Buy or Post Content button press
- Stat numbers silently increment with no tick animation
- Generator row looks identical before and after purchase — nothing signals it is running

related_items.purpose: Core game feel target — what the player experience is supposed to be

acceptance_criteria: Spec where and how the current engagement rate (+X/sec) is displayed — it must be prominent enough to read at a glance
Spec how the rate display reacts at the moment of purchase — the player must be able to see the delta without reading a number carefully (e.g. the rate jumps, flashes, or animates)
Spec button feedback on Buy and Post Content — press state, confirmation flash, or equivalent
Spec how a generator row communicates 'this is actively running' after purchase
The target feeling at purchase is: 'Something just changed. I did that. I can see it working.'

open_questions.question: If the UX treatment doesn't resolve the feel problem, the numbers themselves may need tuning — game-designer should revisit the upgrade delta curve after this ships.

tasks.title: Viral burst event signal — engine contract + trigger
tasks.overview:
The Core Game UI (task #29) implements the visual choreography for the 'going viral' peak moment per UX spec §9 — counter acceleration, screen energy, sustained 5–10s cascade. The UX spec §9.1 explicitly states: 'Viral triggers are determined by the game loop, not this spec. The UX assumes the engine signals a viral burst event with source_generator_id, source_platform_id, duration_ms (5000–10000), magnitude (total bonus engagement).' No such signal exists in the current game loop. The UI has a dormant viralEvent hook ready to be driven — this task decides what fires it and how.

Architect needs to answer: (1) what in the simulation triggers a viral burst? (e.g., follower-rate spike threshold, algorithm state × generator combo, random-chance-on-post gated by state), (2) where in the tick pipeline does it emit, (3) how is the signal surfaced to the UI — is it persisted state on GameState, a transient event queue on the driver, or a one-shot callback? Game-designer owns the frequency target (UX spec §9.5 notes 15–40 minutes between virals per active play session; open question owned by game-designer).

tasks.title: Viral burst visual choreography
tasks.overview:
Implements the full 3-phase visual choreography for the viral burst event on the core game screen. Requires task #50 (viral burst engine) to be complete first — this task wires the UI to the `onViralBurst` driver callback and drives the visual sequence from `GameState.viralBurst.active`.

The choreography has three phases per UX spec §9.2:

Phase 1 — Build (0–1500ms): counter accelerates and shifts to viral gold; source generator row gains pulsing halo; source platform card edges illuminate; background saturation bumps +20%; rising sweep sound begins.

Phase 2 — Peak (1500–end): counter ticks at 3–5× normal rate (real — the engine is producing it); particle burst emits from source generator row drifting toward source platform card (sustained); screen-edge vignette pulses in platform-affinity color at 2s cycle; subtle ±1% zoom pulse on the screen.

Phase 3 — Decay (last 1000–1500ms): tick rate decelerates to baseline; counter color returns to baseline (400ms fade); particles dissipate; edge glow fades; summary badge 'VIRAL +N' appears near counter for 1.5s then fades.

The counter tick-rate drama is the star. All other effects frame it. Per spec §9.3: easing the tick rate across the 3 phases (accelerate → sustain → decelerate) is essential — a flat burst feels cheap.

Note: the edge vignette during viral override uses the source platform's affinity color, not the mood color. This is a state on the same vignette layer as the algorithm mood — they do not stack. See algorithm-mood-visibility.md proposal for the vignette layer architecture.

tasks.title: Prestige cluster (Upgrades + Rebrand buttons) + Phase 1 rebrand modal
tasks.overview:
Replace the current single-button rebrand-corner in GameScreen.tsx (~line 321) with the two-button prestige cluster per ux/prestige-rebrand-screen.md §2.1–2.3. Both buttons are visually grouped (~12px spacing, shared tint/border) and sit bottom-right, visually deferential to the engagement counter and generator list. Before the player reaches the first-rebrand eligibility threshold, both buttons render at 3:1 contrast (not hidden) — seeing them teases the system. Hover tooltips: Upgrades shows current Clout balance (e.g. '42 Clout'); Rebrand shows Clout-on-rebrand preview (e.g. 'Rebrand → +8 Clout') using the existing cloutForRebrand(total_followers) function.

This task also KILLS window.confirm. The existing handleRebrand() uses window.confirm, which violates the project SOP on browser dialogs. Replace it with a modal that renders only Phase 1 of the Rebrand Ceremony (the Threshold Review, per §4.2): a factual account of 'Current followers / Clout earned / New Clout balance' and side-by-side 'What resets' and 'What persists' lists, with Cancel and Continue buttons. Cancel closes the modal and resumes the game loop. Continue calls the existing driver rebrand() method and returns immediately to the main screen (Phases 2–4 land in E4). The game loop MUST pause while the ceremony modal is open (production halts, Algorithm shifts pause) per §4.1.

The Upgrades button opens a Clout Shop modal SHELL for now — header 'Rebrand Upgrades', Clout balance display, × close affordance (plus Esc and backdrop click), but an empty list body ('Upgrades coming soon' or equivalent). The real shop list and purchase flow land in E3. Game loop continues ticking when shop is open (not paused, backdrop ~30% opacity) per §3.1. Keyboard nav: Esc closes both modals, Tab focus-traps while open, Enter confirms primary action.

This chunk must ship a working replacement for window.confirm — no interim state leaves the confirm call in place.

tasks.title: Migrate CloutUpgrade schema to per-level arrays + add missing data entries
tasks.overview:
The CloutUpgrade data model in architecture/core-systems.md was updated to use per-level arrays (`values: float[]`, `lookaheads: int[]`) to support non-uniform effects across levels (e.g. engagement_boost L1=1.5×, L2=2.5×, L3=5×). The current code in client/src/types.ts, client/src/static-data/index.ts, and client/src/game-loop/index.ts still uses the OLD single-scalar schema (`value: number`, `lookahead: number`). This task brings the code up to the architecture spec and populates the full upgrade + post-prestige generator data set per the accepted clout-upgrade-menu.md proposal. This is a pure backend/data task — no player-facing UI changes. It is a prerequisite for E3 (the Clout Shop UI) to correctly display and consume per-level effects.

Scope:

1) Type migration in client/src/types.ts:
   - { type: 'engagement_multiplier'; value: number } → { type: 'engagement_multiplier'; values: number[] }
   - { type: 'algorithm_insight'; lookahead: number } → { type: 'algorithm_insight'; lookaheads: number[] }
   - Keep generator_unlock and platform_headstart as-is (max_level: 1)

2) Update static data entries in client/src/static-data/index.ts:
   - Add `engagement_boost`: cost [10, 30, 75], max_level 3, values [1.5, 2.5, 5.0]
   - Update `algorithm_insight`: cost [15, 40], max_level 2, lookaheads [1, 2]
   - Update `platform_headstart_instasham`: cost [20]
   - Update `platform_headstart_grindset`: cost [50]
   - Remove `platform_headstart_chirper` if present (chirper is starter platform)
   - Add `ai_slop_unlock`: cost [25], effect { type: 'generator_unlock', generator_id: 'ai_slop' }
   - Add `deepfakes_unlock`: cost [60], effect { type: 'generator_unlock', generator_id: 'deepfakes' }
   - Add `algorithmic_prophecy_unlock`: cost [100], effect { type: 'generator_unlock', generator_id: 'algorithmic_prophecy' }

3) Add post-prestige generator definitions to generator static data:
   - `ai_slop`: base_engagement_rate = 8× Viral Stunts, follower_conversion_rate 0.6, trend_sensitivity 0.1
   - `deepfakes`: base_engagement_rate = 15× Viral Stunts, follower_conversion_rate 0.3, trend_sensitivity 0.95
   - `algorithmic_prophecy`: base_engagement_rate = 40× Viral Stunts, follower_conversion_rate 0.5, trend_sensitivity 0.5
   - These generators MUST have NO entry in unlockThresholds.generators (never follower-unlocked; unlocked only via Clout upgrade)

4) Update game-loop effect consumption (client/src/game-loop/index.ts) to index into arrays:
   - engagement_multiplier: cloutBonus reads `effect.values[level - 1]` instead of scalar.
   - algorithm_insight: prestige/index.ts getUpcomingShifts reads `effect.lookaheads[level - 1]`

5) Verify applyRebrand re-applies `generator_unlock` effects after resetting generators to owned:false, per architecture §Prestige post-reset Clout re-application order. Fix if missing. Same for platform_headstart on platform reset.

6) Update tests in client/src/game-loop/index.test.ts, client/src/prestige/index.test.ts, client/src/driver/index.test.ts to match new schema. Add test coverage for post-prestige generator unlocking via applyRebrand.

tasks.title: Clout Shop modal — full upgrade list + purchase flow + states
tasks.overview:
Replace the Clout Shop modal shell from E1 with the full shop per ux/prestige-rebrand-screen.md §3. The shop is ~720×560px, centered, dark-backdrop-at-30%, and MUST NOT pause the game loop — the main screen continues ticking at the modal edges. This is planning, not escape.

Scope:

1) Clout balance header (§3.2): 'Clout: N' top of modal body, 36px/weight-600 numeral, P0 contrast (15.8:1), animates downward (400ms tick) on purchase.

2) Upgrade categories (§3.4), in fixed order: ENGAGEMENT (engagement_multiplier effects), UNLOCKS (generator_unlock + platform_headstart effects merged), INSIGHT (algorithm_insight effect). Within each category, rows sort by ascending cost so next-affordable is near category top.

6) Shop states (§3.6): empty (first visit, Clout:0, all rows locked, header copy 'Earn Clout by rebranding. Your first rebrand is ahead.'), partial (default), all-maxed (footer 'You\'ve mastered the system. Rebrand to reset the stage.').

Gate by same Reduce Motion detection used in core-game-screen.md §12.

8) Accessibility: CVD-safe (no color-only signals — text labels ✓/MAX/locked/cost/glyph pair with state), P0/P1/P2 contrasts per role standards, ⬆ button ≥44×44px hit area, full keyboard nav, focus trap while modal open.

tasks.title: Rebrand Ceremony — Phases 2 (Eulogy), 3 (Commit), 4 (Dissolution→Rebirth)
tasks.overview:
Extend E1's minimal Phase 1 modal into the full 4-phase Rebrand Ceremony per ux/prestige-rebrand-screen.md §4.3–4.6. Total runtime ~8s first rebrand (unskipped), ~5s subsequent (eulogy skipped). The ceremony must feel earned — it fires rarely and deserves its runtime. Game loop stays paused for the entire ceremony (established in E1).

Scope:

1) Phase 2 Eulogy (§4.3, ~3–4s, skippable): 3–4 sequential stanzas, each fading in over 600ms and lingering ~1s. Content sources: top platform by followers, top generator by lifetime engagement, longest-held Algorithm state during run, total engagement earned. Stanzas pulled from a data-driven template library with run-state substitution (see open question). Background desaturates 0% → 40% over 3s (linear). Skip link bottom-right: first rebrand (rebrand_count === 0 → 1) HIDES skip until 3rd stanza appears; subsequent rebrands show skip immediately. Tone: affectionate, deadpan, satirical. Auto-advances to Phase 3 1s after final stanza — no manual advance.

2) Phase 3 Commit (§4.4, ~1s): single headline 'Become someone new.' (24px/weight-500), one 280×80px button labeled 'Rebrand' (biggest button in the game). No Cancel button. Backdrop-click/Esc cancels. On tap: button scale 0.96 (80ms), low-register tone (lowest in game), transition to Phase 4.

3) Phase 4 Dissolution → Rebirth (§4.5, ~3.8s):
   - Dissolution (0–2s): backdrop counters (engagement, followers, generators) tick downward rapidly (0→1000ms ease-in), background desaturates to 10% (500→2000ms ease-in), descending-pitch whoosh sound (800→1800ms), screen fades to deep-muted-blue wash (1500→2000ms ease-in).
   - Rebirth (2–3.8s): 400ms held silence on dark wash, single clean opening tone (higher register than commit tone, 2400→2600ms), fade to fresh main screen over 900ms (2600→3500ms ease-out).
   - Phase 4 has NO cancellation — rebrand is committed to state when Phase 4 begins; visual is just playing out.

4) Cancellation behavior (§4.6): Phase 2 backdrop-click/Esc cancels (no visible Cancel). Phase 3 backdrop-click/Esc cancels (no visible Cancel). Phase 4 cannot cancel.

5) Reduce Motion variants (§7): replace counter tick-down dissolution with single 1s fade-to-zero, replace Phase 2 background desaturation with instant, replace Phase 4 desaturation with instant, preserve sound design AND held-silence timing AND fade-back timing (timing is not motion).

6) Screen reader support (§7): Phase 2 stanzas render with aria-live='polite'. Phase 4 announces 'Rebranding. Run N beginning.' via aria-live.

7) Call driver.rebrand() at the Commit tap (transition from Phase 3 to Phase 4) — this commits the rebrand to state. Phase 4 visual plays out after state is committed.

tasks.title: Remove post-prestige generators from main generator list
tasks.overview:
Post-prestige generators (ai_slop, deepfakes, algorithmic_prophecy) are currently rendered in the main generator list in GameScreen, below Viral Stunts, each showing a — x1.00 multiplier and a 'Buy' action priced in engagement (e.g. 'Buy AI Slop — cost: 200.00M engagement'). This contradicts the accepted spec ux/prestige-rebrand-screen.md §3.1 and §3.4, which places these generators in the Clout Shop modal under the UNLOCKS category, priced in Clout, purchased via the Upgrades button in the prestige cluster (bottom-right).

The Clout Shop is a separate planned task (see the existing 'Clout Shop modal — full upgrade list + purchase flow + states' build task). Until it lands, post-prestige generators should simply NOT appear in the main generator list at all. When the Clout Shop ships, they will render there as UNLOCKS rows per spec.

This is a surgical filter change: the generator list rendering logic should exclude any generator whose id is in the postPrestige set (already defined in save/index.ts as ['ai_slop', 'deepfakes', 'algorithmic_prophecy']).

The three symptoms this fixes (all currently visible on screen):
1. The locked state reads as broken — '— x1.00' is ambiguous between 'no multiplier' and 'row failed to load'
2. No PRESTIGE band label — these rows sit below the LATE band in visual no-man's-land
3. Cost is denominated in engagement without any engagement-pool indicator visible on the main screen, so the player has no way to reason about proximity to unlock

All three evaporate when the generators stop rendering here. They will re-emerge in the correct surface (Clout Shop, in Clout) when that task ships.

tasks.title: Creator Kit purchase handler
tasks.overview: Implement the kit-purchase action. The handler validates the purchase, spends engagement, and increments the player's level for that item. It MUST expose a post-purchase hook (or otherwise be extensible) so E6 (Phone mid-run head-start) can cleanly layer in without rewriting the handler. Steps per the architecture spec §Purchase: (1) validate player.creator_kit[item_id] < KitItemDef.max_level, (2) validate player.engagement >= KitItemDef.cost[current_level], (3) decrement engagement, (4) increment level. Reject the action if either validation fails; do not mutate state. Follow the existing UI→game-loop action dispatch pattern used for Clout purchases.

tasks.title: Generator level growth curves: cost parity + L=10 cap + runtime clamps + MAX UI
tasks.overview:
Implement the single coherent correctness fix from the accepted generator-level-growth-curves proposal. Restores cost/reward parity (cost now uses levelMultiplier, not 4^(L-1)), installs a hard L=10 cap per generator, adds runtime clamps that prevent the save-breaking Infinity bug from ever re-emerging, and swaps the Lvl↑ affordance for a MAX label at cap. All changes ship together per architect review — cost parity without the cap still overflows at L~72, and the runtime clamps are permanent invariants that belong next to the formula they guard.

Scope of change (with file:line anchors):

1. DATA CONTRACT — Add `max_level: number` to `GeneratorDef` in client/src/types.ts:358 (mirror the shape of `CloutUpgradeDef.max_level` at types.ts:412). Update the stale doc comment at types.ts:390 that still encodes the old `4^(currentLevel-1)` formula. Seed `max_level: 10` on every generator entry in client/src/static-data/index.ts (10 generators total — selfies, memes, tiktok_dances, podcasts, youtube_shorts, onlyfans, viral_stunts, ai_slop, deepfakes, algorithmic_prophecy). Also update the header comment at static-data/index.ts:33 that encodes the old cost formula.

2. COST FORMULA — Swap `generatorUpgradeCost` at client/src/generator/index.ts:59 to `ceil(base_upgrade_cost × levelMultiplier(targetLevel))` where `targetLevel = currentLevel + 1`. Update the function doc comment. Add at-cap guard: throw (or return a sentinel the callers already expect) when `currentLevel >= def.max_level`. Add the same at-cap guard to `upgradeGenerator` at generator/index.ts:166.

3. RUNTIME CLAMPS (permanent invariants — keep regardless of migration) —
   a) In client/src/game-loop/index.ts:78, clamp `level` input to `levelMultiplier` to [1, 20] before the Math.pow call. Do NOT throw when level > 20 — silently clamp, because this is belt-and-braces for corrupted saves / future code paths that forget a cap.
   b) Clamp `player.engagement` to `Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER` on every write site. Known sites: game-loop/index.ts:358, game-loop/index.ts:427, game-loop/index.ts:631, model/index.ts:189 (addEngagement), offline/index.ts:203. Simplest shape: a tiny `clampEngagement(value: number): number` helper in model/ that every write routes through.

4. UI — `MAX` label at cap: when `g.level >= def.max_level`, the Lvl↑ affordance in client/src/ui/GeneratorList.tsx (around line 340) shows `MAX` instead of the upgrade cost, and tapping it is a no-op (or the drawer opens but the upgrade button inside UpgradeDrawer.tsx is disabled with a MAX label). Match the visual treatment of Clout upgrade MAX state if one exists in CloutShopModal.tsx — check that file for the established pattern before picking a shape.

Non-goals: do NOT retune base_upgrade_cost seed values (game-designer has filed task #88 for the balance pass). Do NOT add a player-facing 'your save was repaired' notice — game-designer explicitly called for silent clamps. Do NOT raise the cap above 10 (engineer review established that L=14 overflows under the full multiplier stack).

tasks.title: Architecture spec: light-mode theming token system and vignette layer contract
tasks.overview:
The codebase currently uses ad-hoc CSS custom properties declared in :root inside client/src/ui/GameScreen.css (--bg-base, --panel-bg, --panel-border, --text-p0..p3, --accent-*). There is no named token system, no semantic layer, and no structural place for per-state mood-edge colors or a vignette layer binding.

The light-mode flip is the right moment to formalize this. This spec defines the token structure the engineer will implement against. It is a small architecture doc (1–2 pages), not a grand theming framework — its scope is exactly what visual-identity-light-mode and algorithm-mood-visibility require, plus the minimum structure that keeps a dark-mode v2 retrofit from being surgery.

The spec MUST answer:
- Token taxonomy: semantic names (--surface-canvas, --surface-panel, --text-primary, --text-secondary, --text-receded, --border-default, --accent-*) vs. raw values; which tier of token GameScreen.css and components reference
- Token location: index.css vs GameScreen.css vs a new theme module — and why
- Mood-edge token binding: how --mood-color-edge is set per algorithm state (data attribute on root, CSS class, React style prop). Must integrate with existing AlgorithmBackground.tsx which already computes instabilityFactor and will drive viral burst state.
- Vignette layer contract: the DOM contract for the second <div> inside AlgorithmBackground per algorithm-mood-visibility OQ1 resolution — what props it takes, what CSS it renders, how 400ms crossfade + instability opacity scaling + viral burst 3-phase state machine are distributed between React and CSS
- Dark-mode v2 inversion points: name which tokens would swap and which would stay, so a future theme flip is a token-file swap, not a component rewrite. No implementation required — just name the seam.

tasks.title: Review manual-action-ladder proposal
tasks.overview: Review the Manual Action Ladder proposal (draft) from an architecture perspective. This proposal replaces the single Post button with a ladder of content verbs (Chirp, Selfie, Livestream...) where each verb has a three-step lifecycle (unlock → upgrade → automate), and the Automate track reduces a shared cooldown value that governs both the automator's timer AND the player's manual tap rate. The load-bearing architectural question is OQ11: does the ladder run PARALLEL to the existing 7-generator roster, or CONSOLIDATE with it (ladder verbs ARE generators)? The game-designer leans toward consolidation because it inherits existing unlock thresholds, level scaling, platform affinity, algorithm modifiers, and follower-distribution routing. But consolidation is a structural refactor of the Upgrades column's identity, the tick pipeline, and the generator data model. Related structural questions: OQ12 (cooldown vs. continuous-rate production model reconciliation), OQ13 (per-verb algorithm-modifier binding, currently hard-coded to selfies in postClick at game-loop/index.ts L62). This proposal supersedes `generator-balance-and-algorithm-states.md` §Manual Click Value (the CLICK_BASE_ENGAGEMENT=1.0 directive) — architect should weigh in on whether the revised balance target (automators take over per-verb, manual stays supplementary) is defensible.

tasks.title: Resolve manual-action-ladder Unlock-phase balance gaps
tasks.overview:
Two balance/data questions surfaced during architectural planning of the manual-action-ladder that the accepted proposal does not answer. Both block the engineer's implementation of the ladder's Unlock phase.

**Q1 — Threshold unit: followers or engagement?** OQ5's resolution text says 'engagement thresholds' and rationalizes '100 engagement ≈ 40s of tapping' for chirps→selfies pacing. But the existing code (`client/src/generator/index.ts` checkGeneratorUnlocks, `client/src/types.ts` GeneratorDef.unlock_threshold comment 'Total followers required to unlock') reads `unlock_threshold` against `total_followers`, and the numbers in proposal §14d (50/100/200/1k/5k/20k/100k) are IDENTICAL to the existing follower thresholds. Either (a) the rationale text was a slip and thresholds stay follower-based (simplest — no code change); or (b) the ladder semantics genuinely require flipping to engagement-gated unlocks (which needs a new code path and probably a new `lifetime_engagement` or similar field, since `player.engagement` fluctuates with spends and can't be the gate). Please confirm which one you want, and if (b), name what the gate reads — current balance, lifetime earned, or something else.

**Q2 — Unlock cost per ladder verb.** §2 names Unlock as a 'one-time cost' purchase distinct from Automate (count) and Upgrade (level). §14c gives chirps' `base_buy_cost: 2`, but no Unlock cost is authored for selfies / livestreams / podcasts / viral_stunts. Two options: (a) reuse the existing `base_buy_cost` field as the Unlock cost (first Automate purchase would then also cost `base_buy_cost × 1.15^0` = same number, which is fine since count=0→1 is the first Automate level); (b) add a new `unlock_cost` field to GeneratorDef. Architect leans (a) for zero data-model cost — please confirm, and if (a), confirm the existing base_buy_cost values (selfies 10, livestreams 130_000, podcasts 1_400_000, viral_stunts 20_000_000) are the intended Unlock costs; if (b), author the per-verb unlock_cost numbers.

These are small balance/data-model decisions — expect ≤30 min of design thought, then integrate back into manual-action-ladder.md §14 or a short addendum. The engine-refactor engineer task (E2) is NOT blocked on this; the lifecycle-expansion engineer task (E3) IS.

tasks.title: Author manual-action-ladder architecture spec
tasks.overview:
The accepted manual-action-ladder proposal locks the user-facing mechanics and the engine-level contracts (yield/rate split, postClick(verbId), last_manual_click_at field), but several data-model and component-boundary decisions the engineer needs are not in the proposal and must be formalized in an architecture doc at `.frames/sdlc/architecture/manual-action-ladder.md` before engineer tasks E2/E3 start. This is the architect (self) authoring that doc.

**Decisions to formalize (architect's call):**

1. **Post-prestige generator resolution.** Proposal names an 8-generator roster (5 ladder + 3 passive) but the codebase has 3 additional post-prestige generators (ai_slop, deepfakes, algorithmic_prophecy) unlocked via Clout upgrades. Resolution: `manual_clickable: false` for all three; their base_engagement_rate splits backward-compatibly (yield=1.0, rate=existing). Document this so the engineer seeds static-data correctly.

2. **Three-state lifecycle → existing data model mapping.** Proposal §2 defines Unlock → Upgrade → Automate as three distinct purchases. Existing GeneratorState has {owned, level, count} with two purchase actions. Resolution: repurpose `owned` as the 'unlocked by purchase' gate for manual-clickable generators (NOT an automatic flip on threshold-met). For passive-only generators, owned-flip-on-threshold-met stays as today. Document the checkGeneratorUnlocks behavioral split: for manual-clickable, threshold-met → ghost-slot-eligible (UI surface) but NOT auto-owned; for passive-only, threshold-met → owned (unchanged). Document the new driver action `unlockGenerator(verbId)` that pays the Unlock cost and flips owned=true.

3. **Engine-refactor landing strategy.** driver.click() → click(verbId) is a signature break affecting 20+ call sites across UI + tests. Resolution: E2 keeps a temporary `driver.click()` shim that routes to `click('chirps')`, preserving main on green. E4 (UI wiring) removes the shim when it rips out PostZone.tsx for the per-verb ladder. Document this contract and name the removal point explicitly.

4. **Migration must seed generators.chirps.** Adding 'chirps' to the GeneratorId union means existing saves' `Record<GeneratorId, GeneratorState>` is missing a key. migrateV8toV9 must seed BOTH `player.last_manual_click_at = {}` AND `state.generators.chirps = createGeneratorState('chirps', true)` (owned=true since threshold=0). Document.

5. **CLICK_BASE_ENGAGEMENT consumer in GameScreen.tsx.** GameScreen.tsx L24/L138 imports CLICK_BASE_ENGAGEMENT to compute the per-click preview. Document the replacement path: E2 removes the import and passes a per-verb yield to PostZone (stub with chirps yield during the shim window).

6. **Scope of formula change to computeGeneratorEffectiveRate.** Formula becomes `count × base_event_rate × base_event_yield × level_multiplier × algoMod × clout × kit`. Mathematically identical for seeded existing generators. Document the required equivalence test.

**What the spec should include (per architect role required doc structure):**
- **Scope** — one-sentence what this architecture owns, what it does NOT own
- **Data model delta** — every field change (base_engagement_rate split, manual_clickable flag, last_manual_click_at, semantic change to owned for manual-clickable gens, chirps entry in all Record<GeneratorId,...> static-data)
- **Interface contracts** — postClick signature, unlockGenerator signature (new), checkGeneratorUnlocks split behavior
- **Coupling analysis** — where UI, driver, save, static-data, and game-loop couple against these contracts; what breaks if each changes
- **Migration contract** — v8→v9 seed list + invariants
- **Open questions** — any remaining ambiguities (should be ~zero after this spec if D1 resolves cleanly)
- **Assumptions** — load-bearing assumptions flagged (e.g., 'D1 resolves threshold unit to followers — if engagement, checkGeneratorUnlocks needs rework')

The architect SHOULD reference D1's resolution if it's landed by the time this is written; otherwise the spec can note D1 as the load-bearing assumption and be revised once D1 lands.

tasks.title: Author Actions-column manual-action-ladder UX spec
tasks.overview:
The accepted manual-action-ladder proposal replaces the single Post button with a per-verb ladder in the Actions column. The ux-designer's review (proposal lines 546-551) committed to authoring a full Actions-column ladder UX spec as a follow-up task. This is that task.

**What the spec must cover:**

1. **Ghost-slot anatomy** — per OQ6 resolution: one ghost slot at a time (next verb in sequence), silhouette icon + unlock-condition text, 0.35 opacity, ~60px height, not tappable until condition met; on condition-met it opacifies, shows unlock cost, becomes tappable. Spec full visual treatment: typography, spacing, silhouette style, condition-text format ('100 followers' vs '500 engagement' — resolve in concert with D1's threshold-unit resolution), opacity-transition animation on condition-met.

2. **Live-verb-button anatomy** — the permanent, tappable button that replaces the current PostZone for each unlocked verb. Spec: pulse indicator (tied to cooldown), cooldown ring visualization (circular progress indicating time since last tap vs verb's current cooldownMs), automate-level badge (shows count), per-verb iconography (chirps/selfies/livestreams/podcasts/viral_stunts visual identity — may need icon proposals), 80px height (per existing Actions-column standard), yield-per-tap number display. Spec the interaction states (idle, pulse-ready, mid-cooldown, just-tapped).

3. **Spotlight-slot transition animation** — per OQ3 resolution, the most-recently-unlocked verb pins to the top of the scroll region on desktop, to the bottom-anchor position on mobile. Spec the transition when a new verb unlocks: how the previous spotlight demotes, how the new verb animates in, timing + easing.

4. **Mobile anchor dynamics** — the Actions column lives in a bottom-anchor position on mobile. With 5 live verbs + ghost + brand-deal strip, the density exceeds the 4-cap soft limit and triggers internal scroll. Spec how the bottom-anchor slot interacts with scroll (does it stay fixed? does the ghost slot sit above it per OQ6?), and define the scroll affordance (rubber-banding, scroll-gradient, etc).

5. **Empty/edge states** — what does the Actions column look like at session start (only chirps unlocked)? What does it look like at full ladder unlocked (5 live + ghost-disappears-or-gets-replaced-by-end-state)? What's the transition UI when a verb's Automate purchase crosses the cooldown floor (0.01s)?

6. **Accessibility** — 44px iOS tap minimum (proposal references 80px floor); per-verb iconography must meet color-independence contrast targets; cooldown ring must be legible for color-blind users.

This spec feeds directly into engineer task E4 (Actions-column ladder UI wiring). E4 is blocked on this spec — engineer needs visual + interaction detail, not just data contracts, to implement the ladder.

The UX designer can consult game-designer or architect via subagent for any questions about mechanics binding, but the visual/interaction decisions are ux-designer's call.

tasks.title: Engine refactor: yield/rate split, postClick(verbId), chirps seed, v8→v9 migration, click() shim
tasks.overview:
Implement the engine skeleton for the manual-action-ladder per architect's 5-step refactor spec (proposal §12, architect's OQ13 review lines 471-481, architect's re-review cooldown-gate snippet lines 375-383) and the architecture spec authored in A1. This is the pure engine refactor — it does NOT implement the Unlock/Automate lifecycle expansion (E3 does that) or the per-verb UI ladder (E4 does that). After this task lands, main is green with a click-on-chirps shim preserving existing behavior while the lifecycle + UI land incrementally.

**Scope (per architect + engineer's re-review agreement):**

1. **types.ts changes:**
   - Add `'chirps'` as first entry in GeneratorId union (before 'selfies')
   - Add `manual_clickable: boolean` field to GeneratorDef
   - Split GeneratorDef.base_engagement_rate into `base_event_yield: number` + `base_event_rate: number`. May optionally keep base_engagement_rate as a computed getter for grep-insurance during deprecation (engineer's call per proposal line 645).
   - Add `last_manual_click_at: Record<GeneratorId, number>` to Player

2. **static-data/index.ts changes:**
   - Split all 10 existing generator defs' base_engagement_rate into base_event_yield + base_event_rate per proposal §14a (5 ladder verbs — specific yield/rate pairs to hit cooldown targets) and §14b (3 passive-only — yield=1.0, rate=existing). For the 3 post-prestige generators (ai_slop, deepfakes, algorithmic_prophecy), use the backward-compatible seed yield=1.0, rate=existing base_engagement_rate (per A1 architecture spec).
   - Add `manual_clickable` to every generator: true for the 5 ladder verbs (chirps, selfies, livestreams, podcasts, viral_stunts); false for memes, hot_takes, tutorials, ai_slop, deepfakes, algorithmic_prophecy.
   - Add chirps entry to GENERATOR_DEFS with all cells per proposal §14c.
   - Add chirps entries to every PLATFORM_DEFS[platformId].content_affinity (chirper=1.5, instasham=1.0, grindset=0.6 per §14c).
   - Add chirps entries to every ALGORITHM_STATE_DEFS[stateId].state_modifiers per §14c table (short_form_surge=1.7, authenticity_era=0.8, engagement_bait=1.5, nostalgia_wave=1.0, corporate_takeover=0.7).
   - Add chirps to unlockThresholds.generators with value 0.
   - DO NOT change selfies' unlock_threshold numeric value in this task — the threshold semantics decision comes from D1, lifecycle change comes from E3.

3. **game-loop/index.ts changes:**
   - Delete CLICK_BASE_ENGAGEMENT (L55) and CLICK_GENERATOR_ID (L62)
   - Update computeGeneratorEffectiveRate (L156-177) multiplication to: `count × base_event_rate × base_event_yield × level_multiplier × algoMod × clout × kit` — mathematically identical output for seeded existing generators (add a regression test asserting equivalence)
   - Refactor postClick signature: `postClick(state, staticData, verbId: GeneratorId) → GameState`
   - Inside postClick per architect's formula of record (proposal line 635): `earned = def.base_event_yield × level_multiplier(genState.level) × algoMod × clout_bonus × kit_bonus`. No platform affinity term.
   - Add cooldown gate per architect's snippet: `const cooldownMs = 1000 / (Math.max(1, genState.count) * def.base_event_rate); if (now - (state.player.last_manual_click_at[verbId] ?? 0) < cooldownMs) return state;`
   - On success, write `last_manual_click_at[verbId] = now` in the returned state.
   - Reject if generator is not manual_clickable: return state unchanged (don't throw — silent no-op matches click() error model).

4. **driver/index.ts changes:**
   - Expand interface: add `click(verbId: GeneratorId): void` method.
   - Preserve existing `click(): void` as a shim that routes to `click('chirps')` — temporary, removed by E4. Document with a TODO comment referencing E4.
   - `runAction` wrapper unchanged.

5. **save/index.ts changes:**
   - Add migrateV8toV9 that seeds BOTH:
     - `player.last_manual_click_at = {}`
     - `state.generators.chirps = { id: 'chirps', owned: true, level: 1, count: 0 }` (owned=true since chirps threshold=0)
   - Bump CURRENT_VERSION to 9 and add migrateV8toV9 to the chain at L477-479.

6. **model/index.ts changes:**
   - createInitialGameState's existing logic already seeds `owned=threshold===0`. With chirps at threshold=0, it gets owned=true automatically. Verify no code change needed here beyond type update.

7. **ui/display.ts changes:**
   - Add chirps to GENERATOR_ORDER (position 0, before 'selfies')
   - Add chirps entry to GENERATOR_DISPLAY with name/icon/category/color/tagline (placeholder values are fine; UX may update in E4)

8. **ui/GameScreen.tsx changes:**
   - Remove CLICK_BASE_ENGAGEMENT import (L24)
   - Replace the perClick computation (L138) with a per-verb yield lookup stubbed to chirps' yield during the shim window (to be refactored in E4 for the full ladder). Implementation note: compute `def.base_event_yield * levelMultiplier(genState.level) * algoMod * clout` for verbId='chirps'.
   - Update L134-154 context-label code so it uses 'chirps' (or keeps the 'selfies' hardcode during the shim — E4 will rewrite this code path).

9. **Tests:**
   - Update all existing `postClick(state, STATIC_DATA)` call sites in game-loop/index.test.ts to pass a verbId (most will use 'chirps' or 'selfies').
   - Add new tests: cooldown-gate accept/reject paths, verb dispatch by id, yield/rate formula equivalence across existing generators (regression — identical output to pre-split), last_manual_click_at write-back, manual_clickable=false rejection.
   - Update CLICK_BASE_ENGAGEMENT assertion at L524/L532 to express the new per-verb formula.
   - driver.click() shim tests: verify the zero-arg call routes through to click('chirps').
   - All integration.test.ts and driver.test.ts call sites of driver.click() continue working via the shim — no test rewrites required for those.

**Acceptance:** existing generators produce mathematically identical passive output; existing `click()` call sites continue to work via shim; new `click(verbId)` dispatches correctly per verb; save v8 → v9 migration preserves existing state + seeds new fields; all tests pass.

**Not in scope (deferred to E3/E4):**

- selfies threshold change 0 → 100 (E3, because it's tied to the lifecycle change and D1's balance resolution)

tasks.title: Unlock-as-purchase lifecycle: new unlock action, checkGeneratorUnlocks split, selfies threshold change
tasks.overview:
Extend the engine (post-E2) to implement the three-state lifecycle (Unlock → Upgrade → Automate) per proposal §2. This adds a distinct Unlock purchase action for manual-clickable generators, splits checkGeneratorUnlocks behavior, and applies D1's balance resolution for threshold unit + unlock-cost values. After this task lands, the data model supports the full lifecycle; E4 wires the UI.

**Scope (per A1 architecture spec + D1 balance resolution):**

1. **checkGeneratorUnlocks split behavior:**
   - For `manual_clickable: false` generators: threshold-met → `owned = true` (today's behavior, unchanged).
   - For `manual_clickable: true` generators: threshold-met → the generator becomes *eligible* for Unlock purchase, but `owned` stays false. A separate player action flips owned=true.
   - The UI uses owned-flag + manual_clickable + threshold-met to decide: show live-verb-button (owned=true), show ghost-slot (manual_clickable && threshold-met && !owned), hide entirely (!threshold-met).

2. **New action: unlockGenerator(state, verbId, staticData) → GameState** (in `generator/index.ts`):
   - Throw if generator is not manual_clickable (passive-only gens use buyGenerator).
   - Throw if threshold not met (gate check).
   - Throw if already owned.
   - Throw if insufficient engagement.
   - Deduct unlock cost (field to use determined by D1 — `base_buy_cost` if D1 resolves to 'reuse', or new `unlock_cost` field if D1 resolves to 'new field').
   - Set `generators[verbId].owned = true`.

3. **buyGenerator contract change for manual-clickable gens:**
   - Today: requires owned=true before count++ (and owned auto-flips on threshold-met).
   - Under the new lifecycle: still requires owned=true, but owned now only flips via explicit unlockGenerator action (for manual-clickable). Error message must be updated to point at 'this verb needs to be unlocked first' vs today's 'not yet unlocked — check follower thresholds'.
   - For passive-only (manual_clickable=false): behavior unchanged.

4. **New driver action: driver.unlock(verbId: GeneratorId): void:**
   - Add to GameDriver interface.
   - Route through runAction wrapper with context `{ verbId }` and action name `'unlock'`.
   - Add 'unlock' to the ActionName union.

5. **Static-data threshold change:**
   - selfies unlock_threshold: 0 → 100 (per proposal §14d). The unit (followers vs engagement) is D1's call; the numeric value is locked per the proposal.
   - If D1 resolves to engagement-based thresholds, checkGeneratorUnlocks is further extended to gate on the named source (current engagement vs. lifetime engagement vs. other). A new Player.lifetime_engagement field may be required.

6. **Initial-state seeding:**
   - createInitialGameState today sets `owned = (threshold === 0)`. With selfies at threshold=100, selfies is no longer owned at start. Chirps (threshold=0) is owned. Verify this is the intended new-player starting state per the proposal's aesthetic (§10): player starts with chirps only, unlocks selfies at 100 followers (or engagement per D1).

7. **Tests:**
   - unlockGenerator: success path (deducts cost, flips owned=true); reject if passive-only, not threshold-met, already owned, insufficient engagement.
   - checkGeneratorUnlocks split: passive-only still auto-flips owned; manual-clickable does NOT auto-flip owned.
   - buyGenerator error message change for manual-clickable gens needing unlock first.
   - driver.unlock wiring: runAction wrapping, error surface.
   - Initial-state: fresh game has chirps owned, selfies not owned.
   - Migration doesn't retroactively un-own selfies for existing saves — players who had selfies owned under old rules keep it (migrateV8toV9 does not touch owned flags).

**Not in scope (deferred to E4):**
- UI rendering of ghost-slot, unlock-cost display, unlock button.
- UX ladder replacement of PostZone.
- Removing the driver.click() shim.

tasks.title: Actions-column ladder UI wiring: per-verb ladder + ghost slot + spotlight + remove click() shim
tasks.overview:
Replace the single-button PostZone with the per-verb Actions-column ladder per U1's UX spec, bound to the engine surfaces delivered by E2 (postClick(verbId), click(verbId) driver action) and E3 (unlockGenerator, ghost-slot eligibility via manual_clickable + threshold-met + !owned). Remove the driver.click() shim as part of this task — all call sites migrate to click(verbId).

**Scope:**

1. **Replace PostZone.tsx with a ladder component** (or refactor it into one) rendering per U1's spec:
   - Live verb buttons: one per owned && manual_clickable generator, with pulse indicator (tied to cooldown), cooldown ring (time since last tap vs verb's current cooldownMs), automate-level badge (count), per-verb iconography.
   - Ghost slot: one slot at a time, showing the next manual-clickable verb that is threshold-met && !owned (from E3's split). Opacity 0.35 until condition-met, then opacifies and becomes tappable. Tap invokes driver.unlock(verbId).
   - Spotlight slot: most-recently-unlocked verb pinned to top on desktop, bottom-anchor on mobile. Requires tracking 'most recently unlocked' — either derive from existing state (order of first-owned events, possibly via last_manual_click_at timestamp or new Player field) OR add a minimal Player.last_unlocked_verb_id field. Engineer's call after reading U1.
   - Empty state: chirps-only ladder + selfies ghost slot.
   - Mobile: scroll region + bottom-anchor interaction per U1.

2. **Per-verb tap handler → driver.click(verbId):**
   - Each live-verb-button's onClick routes to `driver.click(verbId)`.
   - useGame.ts 'click' export becomes `click: (verbId) => driver.click(verbId)` (typed).

3. **Per-verb yield preview:**
   - GameScreen.tsx L138's perClick computation is now per-button, computed per-verb using the same formula as postClick's earned: `def.base_event_yield × levelMultiplier(genState.level) × algoMod × clout × kit`. Extract this as a shared helper (or import from game-loop if already exposed) to keep parity with postClick.

4. **GameScreen.tsx L134-154 context-label rework:**
   - The hardcoded 'Selfies → Chirper' context label presumed a single click target. Under the ladder, either (a) remove the single context label (each button carries its own yield/platform hint), (b) show context for the verb most recently tapped, or (c) show context for the spotlight verb. Engineer's call — consult U1 for the UX intent.

5. **Remove driver.click() shim:**
   - Delete the zero-arg click() method from GameDriver interface and implementation.
   - Update all call sites across: useGame.ts, DebugApp.tsx, integration.test.ts, driver/index.test.ts, game-loop/index.test.ts. TypeScript compiler will surface every site. Most tests should pass a verbId like 'chirps' or 'selfies'; production UI call sites should pass the verbId from the button context.

6. **Cooldown display state:**
   - Cooldown ring animation requires a per-verb reactive render. Options: (a) subscribe to state and recompute remaining cooldown per render (driver already notifies state changes on every tick); (b) use a local requestAnimationFrame loop inside each verb button. Performance note: the UI re-renders ~10× per second already (tick notifies state subscribers). Option (a) is simplest, no extra render loop.

7. **Tests:**
   - Component tests for the ladder: ghost-slot rendering, live-verb-button rendering, tap → driver dispatch, cooldown-ring visualization.
   - Update all test call sites of driver.click() to pass a verbId.
   - Snapshot or screenshot tests if the project uses them (check existing PostZone tests).

tasks.overview: Implement the unblocked sections of the game-screen HUD refinements spec. Three concrete fixes observed in the live GameScreen on 2026-04-05: (1) the top bar has three text layers z-stacking into each other, clobbering the title, the 'followers' label, and overlaying a '↑ kit' unit label on top of the primary currency; (2) number formatting is inconsistent — generator rows use SI notation (868.43M/s) while platform cards emit raw digits (+127097600.7/s), a trust-signal failure; (3) platform-card follower counts render with strikethrough/icon-overlap corruption next to influencer icons. Sections 2 (multiplier pill magnitude encoding) and 3 (BUY vs LVL↑ hierarchy) are NOT in scope — they are blocked on game-designer sign-off. Multiplier pill contrast (§2.2) IS in scope — it is an accessibility floor fix with no open question.

tasks.title: Decide multiplier-pill magnitude encoding + LVL↑ status-line threshold
tasks.overview: Two open questions from the game-screen-hud-refinements UX spec need game-designer sign-off before the remaining sections can be implemented. Both are small, self-contained calls. (1) Multiplier pills currently show the same visual for ×0.96 and ×0.90 — magnitude of effect is invisible at a glance. Spec proposes two options for encoding magnitude. (2) The LVL↑ button column currently shows a price label at full weight regardless of affordability, competing with the BUY button. Spec proposes that when the player is <50% of the level-up cost, LVL↑ drops its button chrome entirely and becomes a flat status line ('LVL 7 · next: 99.84B'). The 50% tip-over is a gameplay-feel call.

related_items.purpose: The spec — §2.3 (magnitude encoding options) and §3.2 (LVL↑ threshold) contain the full framing of both questions.

acceptance_criteria: Decision recorded: multiplier magnitude encoding — Option A (chevron count: 1/2/3 chevrons by |Δ| banding) or Option B (pill fill opacity mapped to |Δ|). Write the decision and rationale directly into §2.3 of the spec.

Decision recorded: LVL↑ flat-status-line threshold — confirm 50% of level-up cost as the tip-over point, or specify an alternative (earlier for more chrome visibility, later for longer quiet periods). Write the decision and rationale directly into §3.2 of the spec.
Both decisions moved from the Open Questions table (§5) to their respective sections so the engineer can implement without ambiguity.

tasks.title: Balance pass: chirps base_buy_cost and early-game payback curve
tasks.overview: Chirps' current balance cells (base_buy_cost=2, base_event_yield=1, base_event_rate=2.5) produce a 0.8-second payback on the first automator purchase — far faster than any other generator (selfies is ~10s). At 2.5 eng/sec passive output for a cost of 2 engagement, the player can chain-buy chirps automators almost instantly, which makes the early game feel trivially cheap. The values were seeded from proposal §14c but feel overpowered in practice. Needs a game-designer eye on whether base_buy_cost should increase, base_event_rate should decrease, or both.

tasks.title: Engine formula refactor + data model + migration + static data retune for level-driven cooldown
tasks.overview:
Implement the core engine changes from the level-driven-manual-cooldown proposal. This is the foundation task — every other task in this plan depends on it (directly or transitively). The proposal redefines: LVL UP drives cooldown (speed), BUY drives yield multiplier (power), and a new `autoclicker_count` field drives passive production. The change surface spans 9 of the engineer's 10 traced code sites (all except the new buyAutoclicker action, which is E2).

Key formula changes:
- `computeGeneratorEffectiveRate`: leading `count` → `autoclicker_count`, `levelMultiplier(level)` → `(1 + count)`, early-return guard `count <= 0` → `autoclicker_count <= 0`
- `postClick` cooldown: `1000 / (Math.max(1, genState.count) * def.base_event_rate)` → `Math.max(50, 1000 / (genState.level * def.base_event_rate))`
- `postClick` earned: `def.base_event_yield * levelMultiplier(genState.level) * algoMod * clout * kit` → `def.base_event_yield * (1 + genState.count) * algoMod * clout * kit`
- Passive tick formula becomes: `autoclicker_count × base_event_rate × base_event_yield × (1 + count) × algoMod × clout × kit`

`levelMultiplier` function stays — still used in `generatorUpgradeCost` for cost scaling. Do not delete it.

The offline calculator (`offline/index.ts`) calls `computeAllGeneratorEffectiveRates` which inherits the formula change — no separate code change needed there, but verify with a test.

tasks.title: Audience mood gate change + AUTOCLICKERS_AFFECT_MOOD killswitch
tasks.overview:
Update the applyTickPosts gate in audience-mood/index.ts so autoclicker fires contribute to audience mood pressure. The current gate (line 295) skips generators where `count <= 0`. Under the level-driven-cooldown model, a generator with count=0 but autoclicker_count > 0 has active autoclickers producing engagement. The gate changes to `count <= 0 && autoclicker_count <= 0`.

Per the game-designer's resolution: autoclicker fires are semantically identical to manual taps — the audience doesn't distinguish who posted. Implementation must include a killswitch boolean flag (`AUTOCLICKERS_AFFECT_MOOD`) in static data or as a module-level constant, so this behavior can be toggled without a code change if playtesting reveals the pressure is wrong.

tasks.title: UI wiring for autoclicker model: purchase button, floating numbers, badge update
tasks.overview:
Wire the autoclicker model into the UI layer. This is the final integration task — it makes the three-axis investment model (LVL UP / BUY / Autoclicker) visible and interactive to the player.

Three sub-deliverables:
1. **Autoclicker purchase button** — location and layout per U2's design decision. Wires to driver.buyAutoclicker(verbId) from E2. Shows cost, count badge.
2. **Floating number emissions for autoclicker fires** — per U1's density/stagger spec. Engine fires in burst mode; UX layer may visually stagger. Need density cap and visual differentiation rules from U1.
3. **Badge semantics update** — the ×N badge on verb buttons changes meaning per U1's spec (likely shows autoclicker_count, not BUY count). Yield display formula changes to show `base_event_yield × (1 + count)` instead of `base_event_yield × levelMultiplier(level)`.

This task depends on E2 (buyAutoclicker action exists), U1 (UX spec for badge/floating numbers/yield display), and U2 (purchase button location decision).

tasks.title: Design ratio-scaled manual tap floating numbers (size + brightness by significance)
tasks.overview:
When the player taps a verb action button, the floating +N number currently pops at fixed size (22px) and fixed color (gold #FFD66B) regardless of value. This proposal: scale the float's font-size and color brightness based on `perClick / currentEngagement` — the ratio of what the tap earned to the player's current balance. Significant taps (high ratio) render large and bright gold. Insignificant taps (low ratio) render small and dim gold. The hue stays gold throughout — no green/yellow/red gradient, because gold is the established 'you earned something' color and introducing hue shifts creates a judgment signal that undermines the reward beat.

Key design decisions needed:
- Min/max font size (e.g., 14px–32px) and the log-scale mapping from ratio to size
- Gold brightness range (dim gold floor → bright gold ceiling) — must maintain WCAG contrast at the dim end
- Whether the size floor (smallest float) is perceptually legible at endgame against busy autoclicker animations
- How this interacts with task #136's autoclicker floating number spec — manual floats must remain visually distinct from autoclicker floats at ALL sizes, so the player always knows 'this was my hand'
- Reduced-motion fallback (size scaling is fine under reduced-motion; brightness scaling is fine; no new animation needed)

Game-designer alignment: supports the mechanic, endorsed gold-brightness over green→yellow, flagged that the player's hand should never become perceptually invisible. Hard design principle: manual tap floats stay distinct from autoclicker floats at every size.

tasks.title: Implement ratio-scaled manual tap floating numbers
tasks.overview:
Scale the verb-float's font-size and gold brightness based on tap significance (perClick / currentEngagement). The proposal is accepted and the engineer confirmed ~30 min implementation. The change is contained to ActionsColumn.tsx (inline style on the verb-float span) and GameScreen.css (defaults become overridable).

Formulas:
- ratio = perClick / max(1, currentEngagement)
- t = clamp((log10(ratio) + 6) / 6, 0, 1)
- fontSize = lerp(16, 32, t)
- saturation = lerp(40, 100, t), lightness = lerp(52, 71, t)
- color = hsl(43, saturation%, lightness%)

Autoclicker floats (§4.6) scale to 80% of the current manual float size. Density-cap batched floats stay fixed at 22px.

tasks.title: Define per-tier color ramp for compact number suffixes (K through Dc)
tasks.overview: The compact number formatter uses suffix tiers (K, M, B, T, Qa, Qi, Sx, Sp, Oc, No, Dc) to denote magnitude. Currently all suffixes render in the same text color. The game-designer wants each tier to carry a color that communicates magnitude — the color should feel like the number is 'getting bigger' as you climb the ladder. Intent is a progressive ramp from muted/cool at K to intense/warm at Dc, so the player can read magnitude at a glance from color alone without parsing the suffix letters. This is a visual-only change — the formatter stays a pure string function, but the UI rendering layer applies color to the suffix portion of the formatted number.

tasks.title: Autoclicker floats: max 6 individual pops, batch with ×N suffix above 6
tasks.overview:
Change the autoclicker floating number emission logic in ActionsColumn.tsx. Currently, individual floats show at ≤8 autoclickers and a single batched float shows at >8. New behavior: always emit `min(autoclicker_count, 6)` floats, staggered across the burst window. When autoclicker_count > 6, each float shows the total engagement for its batch with a ×N suffix where N = ceil(autoclicker_count / 6). The ×N is a feel indicator — total engagement earned per burst is still computed from the real formula and divided evenly across the 6 floats.

Examples:
- 4 autoclickers: 4 floats, each +24, no suffix
- 6 autoclickers: 6 floats, each +24, no suffix
- 8 autoclickers: 6 floats, each +48 ×2
- 18 autoclickers: 6 floats, each +72 ×3
- 60 autoclickers: 6 floats, each +240 ×10

The ×N suffix appears only when ceil(autoclicker_count / 6) > 1. Each float's engagement value = (total burst engagement / 6), so the numbers are honest even though the batching is approximate.

related_items.purpose: Autoclicker float emission logic — the burst handler that creates FloatItems
§4.6 autoclicker floating-number spec — this task supersedes the density cap at 8

acceptance_criteria: At 1–6 autoclickers: that many individual floats, no ×N suffix
At 7+ autoclickers: exactly 6 floats, each showing (total burst engagement / 6) with ×N suffix where N = ceil(autoclicker_count / 6)
×N suffix only appears when N > 1
Floats are staggered across the burst window per existing stagger timing logic
Visual differentiation from manual taps preserved (80% size, 0.7 opacity per §4.6)
Reduced motion: autoclicker floats still suppressed per existing behavior
npm run build succeeds; full test suite passes

tasks.title: Apply level multiplier to autoclicker passive rate
tasks.overview: LVL UP currently only speeds manual taps. Change the passive rate formula so autoclickers also benefit from level. The formula in computeGeneratorEffectiveRate changes from `autoclicker_count × base_event_rate × base_event_yield × (1 + count) × clout × kit` to `autoclicker_count × level × base_event_rate × base_event_yield × (1 + count) × clout × kit`. This is an experimental change — game-designer wants to feel how it plays before committing to it permanently.

Update passive rate assertions to expect level × old values

acceptance_criteria: computeGeneratorEffectiveRate multiplies by generator.level (passive formula includes level factor)

Existing tests updated to reflect new formula
All tests pass, build succeeds

tasks.title: Implement per-tier color ramp for compact number suffixes
tasks.overview:
Apply the accepted tier color ramp to compact number suffixes (K through Dc). The suffix portion of formatted numbers gets colored by magnitude tier — grey at K, deepening blue through M/B/T, violet-purple through Qa/Qi/Sx, hot magenta through Sp/Oc/No/Dc. The number itself stays in default text color; only the suffix letter(s) carry the tier color.

This requires splitting the formatter output so the rendering layer can wrap the suffix in a colored span. The formatter itself (`fmtCompact`/`fmtCompactInt`) stays a pure string function — add a companion function that returns `{ number: string, suffix: string, tierIndex: number }` or similar, so call sites can render the suffix with the appropriate color.

Tier colors apply everywhere compact numbers display EXCEPT floating numbers on verb buttons (those stay gold per game-designer decision).

The color tokens should be added to tokens.css as `--tier-k` through `--tier-dc`.

related_items.purpose: Accepted proposal — full color ramp table, contrast ratios, collision avoidance, application rules

Engagement counter, follower count — render suffix with tier color
Rate display, cost labels — render suffix with tier color
Follower count, rate — render suffix with tier color
Yield display gets tier color. Floating numbers do NOT — stay gold

acceptance_criteria: 11 tier color tokens added to tokens.css: --tier-k (#6E6E6E) through --tier-dc (#B00840) per the accepted proposal table
A structured formatter companion exists that returns { number, suffix, tierIndex } or equivalent — fmtCompact/fmtCompactInt stay unchanged as pure string functions
All compact number displays render the suffix in the tier color and the number in default text color
Floating numbers on verb buttons (ActionsColumn verb-float) do NOT get tier colors — they stay gold/white
All tier colors pass 4.5:1 contrast against #FAF8F5 (verified via accessibility-checker.sh ramp)
Tier colors do not apply to non-suffix text (e.g., the number portion, labels, etc.)
Reduced motion: no change needed (colors are static, not animated)
npm run build succeeds; full test suite passes

tasks.title: Manual tap yield scales with autoclicker count
tasks.overview: Manual taps don't benefit from autoclicker hires. A player with 10 autoclickers watches their army outpace their hand — hiring feels like replacement, not empowerment. Fix: multiply manual tap yield by (1 + autoclicker_count) so the player's hand scales with their team. In postClick, earned changes from `base_event_yield × (1 + count) × clout × kit` to `base_event_yield × (1 + count) × (1 + autoclicker_count) × clout × kit`.

related_items.purpose: postClick — add (1 + genState.autoclicker_count) factor to the earned formula

acceptance_criteria: postClick earned formula is: base_event_yield × (1 + count) × (1 + autoclicker_count) × clout × kit
